title: BaseDef
description: Static definition for a base TYPE (Capital or a specific Unique base) — the template every live Base instance of this type is created from. See game-design/02-bases-and-buildings.md for base types/reference. Live per-match state (ownerId, hqLevel, hexCoord, buildings, walls, population) is NOT part of this schema — see BaseInstance in game-design/07-data-architecture.md.

id.description: Unique identifier for this base type, e.g. "capital", "fort_irongrad".

baseType.description: Capital is per-player at match start; all others are Unique — at most one instance of each exists on a given map.

isCapital.description: A player's owned bases can include multiple Capitals simultaneously; the win condition checks whether one player's owned bases cover every Base where isCapital is true.

terrainException.description: Treehouse buildings may be placed on Forest tiles; Windy Peaks buildings may be placed on Hill tiles. Omitted for every other base type (Plains-only). Individual buildings still declare their own placementRequirement (see data/buildings/schema.json) — this field is a base-level summary of that exception.

uniqueBuildings.description: Building ids unique to this base type (e.g. Fort Irongrad -> ["tank_plant", "grenade_tower"]). Empty/omitted for Capital, which has no specialty building beyond Command Centre.

initialGarrison.description: Only relevant for Unique bases before capture — the template of standing troops present at match start, restricted to unit types this base's own production building(s) can make. Instantiated into real TroopInstance/SquadInstance records at world-gen; this is the authored template, not the live count.

resourceModifiers.description: Structured production multipliers for this base type — replaces an earlier free-text resourceBonus string, which couldn't represent a per-building-type exception. Entries stack multiplicatively, building-scoped first, then base-scoped. E.g. Capital -> [{scope: "base", multiplier: 1.5}, {scope: "building", buildingType: "oil_rig", multiplier: 0.5}], meaning a Capital's Oil Rig nets 1.0 * 0.5 * 1.5 = 0.75x a Unique base's equivalent-level Oil Rig (see game-design/03-resources.md's Oil Rig Notes). Omitted/empty if none.

scope.description: "base" applies to all of this base's resource production; "building" applies only to one building type (requires buildingType).

buildingType.description: Required when scope is "building" — the building id this multiplier applies to, e.g. "oil_rig".
